mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in CPIAUCSL signals easing inflation pressure, which lowers expected future policy restraint and supports equity valuation multiples, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if CPIAUCSL declines but SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in a majority of observed cases, or if the signal's Rank IC stays below 0.03 in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over three months, labor-market deterioration signals weakening final demand and margin risk, which should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if threshold crossings are followed by positive SPX 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or if conditional Rank IC is not negative in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates broad employment and retail demand strength, which supports earnings expectations and should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-strength condition does not outperform either series alone in walk-forward tests, or if 60-day forward returns are not positive in at least 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in INDPRO relative to its recent trend signals stronger industrial supply utilization and cyclical growth, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if upward moves in INDPRO are not followed by positive SPX 20-day returns more often than baseline, or if the signal's direction flips in a rolling 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS rate above 4.5% indicates restrictive policy that compresses financial conditions and equity multiples, so the next 5 trading days should show negative S&P 500 returns when the threshold is crossed.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS above the threshold is not associated with negative 5-day returns in a majority of instances, or if the conditional edge disappears for any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS count together with a rising UNRATE indicates labor-market contraction, which weakens income growth expectations and should generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint deterioration does not produce negative 60-day returns more often than not, or if the interaction term adds no explanatory power versus single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 grows faster than its prior quarter while RSAFS also improves, broad real activity and consumption demand are accelerating, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if concurrent GDP and retail acceleration fails to predict positive 60-day returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the signal is not stable across two consecutive annual windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-disinflation recovery, a low and stable CPIAUCSL combined with an easing FEDFUNDS path supports lower real discount rates and risk appetite, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined low-inflation and easing-policy condition does not outperform unconditional returns, or if positive 20-day returns fail to occur more often than baseline during the specified regime.
